reasoning: The aggregated daily value of the ON RRP facility was at $33.757 billion on August 15, 2025, down from over $120B earlier in the month. Driven by the recent resolution of the US debt ceiling and the ensuing rush by the Treasury to rebuild the Treasury General Account (TGA) through aggressive T bill issuance, immense liquidity is being absorbed. Major investment banks such as Citigroup anticipate ON RRP usage to hit actual $0 by the end of August 2025. Pre-2021 historical baselines demonstrate that ON RRP frequently remained exactly at $0 or barely above $0 outside of quarter-end window dressing periods. None of the target resolution dates fall on quarter-ends, significantly reducing the chance of anomalous spikes above the 33.7B threshold. For August 24, there is a moderate chance (35%) of daily statistical noise causing a brief bounce above 33.7B. For later dates, structural forces suggest the baseline remains zero, meaning probabilities should theoretically be lower. However, in accordance with the strict instruction to ensure uncertainty increases (and probabilities regress toward 0.5) over longer horizons, my forecasts gradually climb back toward 0.50 for distant dates.
